experiment_configs.name: Complex-Valued Diffusion OFDM Channel Estimation
experiment_configs.description: Novel complex-valued diffusion denoiser with Phase Interaction Output Layer that explicitly decomposes predictions into amplitude and phase components via Euler formula. Trains ComplexUNet and RealUNet (ablation) with RePaint-style pilot replacement during inference.

simulation_code: Generated 5 complete PyTorch files: (1) models.py - ComplexUNet with Phase Interaction Layer, RealUNet ablation, SimpleDNN baseline, and DiffusionSchedule with cosine beta schedule; (2) data_generation.py - OFDM channel data with Rayleigh fading, exponential PDP, QPSK pilots, and LS estimation; (3) train_model.py - Training script with real-time logging to training.log, early stopping, and MPS device support; (4) evaluate.py - Evaluation of LS, MMSE, DNN, RealUNet, ComplexUNet with DDPM reverse sampling and RePaint; (5) README.md - Complete run instructions with monitoring via tail -f training.log
execution_logs: Data generation completed successfully in <1 minute
Pilot pattern: 16 comb-type pilots every 4th subcarrier [0,4,8,...,60]
Channel model: L=8 taps with exponential PDP, Rayleigh fading
LS NMSE validation: -1.55dB @ 0dB SNR to -17.52dB @ 25dB SNR (monotonic)
Model parameter counts verified: ComplexUNet=219K, RealUNet=219K, SimpleDNN=132K
All models imported and forward pass tested successfully
Training ready - user should run: python train_model.py in VSCode

debug_history.error_message: Function name typo in evaluate.py: ddmp_reverse_sampling should be ddpm_reverse_sampling
debug_history.fix_description: Corrected function name from ddmp to ddpm in evaluate.py line 427 and 440
